taskDescription: Drag the correct anatomical terms into the blanks to complete statements about thoracic anatomy.
textField:
True ribs (ribs *1-7*) attach directly to the sternum via costal cartilages, while false ribs (8-10) attach indirectly. Floating ribs (*11-12*) have free anterior ends with no sternal attachment. There are *12* pairs of ribs in total.

The sternal angle (angle of *Louis*) is the junction of the manubrium and body of the sternum. It is located at the level of the *T4* vertebra and marks the level of the second rib, the bifurcation of the *trachea*, and the arch of the aorta. It is an important clinical landmark for rib *counting*.

The intercostal neurovascular bundle runs in the costal groove in the order *VAN* (vein, artery, nerve) from superior to inferior. The intercostal nerve runs *inferior* to the artery. A chest drain should be inserted just above the upper border of the rib to avoid damage to the *neurovascular* bundle.

The diaphragm is the primary muscle of *inspiration* and is supplied by the *phrenic* nerve (C3, C4, C5). The right crus of the diaphragm forms the oesophageal *hiatus* at the level of T10. The aortic hiatus is at the level of *T12* and the caval opening at *T8*.

In a flail chest, *paradoxical* chest wall movement occurs because a segment moves independently due to multiple adjacent rib *fractures*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revise the bony landmarks and diaphragm.
